params.text:
The total communication approach

Total communication means using every available method, together, to help a person understand and be understood. Instead of relying on speech alone, you combine words with gesture, facial expression, pictures, objects, signing, and writing — whatever helps that individual.

  • Whatever works: there is no single right method; you use the mix that suits the person.
  • Two-way: it supports both how you get information across and how the person responds.
  • Person-led: the person's own preferences guide which methods you use.

Non-verbal communication

Most communication is non-verbal. Facial expression, eye contact, gesture, posture, and tone of voice all carry meaning, and for some people they are the main way they communicate. Pay close attention to a person's body language: it may tell you they are in pain, anxious, happy, or ready to end a conversation.

Be aware of the messages your own body sends. A warm expression, an unhurried manner, and an open posture help a person feel safe and listened to. A glance at the clock or a tense stance can say more than your words. Match your tone and pace to the person, and let them see your face clearly.

params.taskDescription: Drag each word into the correct gap to complete the passage about total communication.
params.textField: *Total* communication means using every available method together, combining speech with gesture, pictures, and *signing*. Most communication is *non-verbal*, so a person's body language and facial *expression* often tell you how they feel.
